13: # Quick Reference - Commands, Checklists & Troubleshooting
14: 
15: Fast lookup for spec folder commands, checklists, and troubleshooting.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Quick reference for spec folder management, commands, and troubleshooting using the progressive enhancement model.
24: 
25: ### Progressive Enhancement Model
26: 
27: ```
28: Level 1 (Baseline):     spec.md + plan.md + tasks.md + implementation-summary.md
29: Level 2 (Verification): Level 1 + checklist.md
30: Level 3 (Full):         Level 2 + decision-record.md + optional research
31: Level 3+ (Extended):    Level 3 + governance/AI execution content
32: ```
33: 
34: ### Key Points
35: 
36: - LOC thresholds are **SOFT GUIDANCE** (not enforcement)
37: - **Enforcement is MANUAL** - verify required templates exist before claiming completion
38: 
39: ---
40: 
41: ## 2. LEVEL DECISION SHORTCUTS (PROGRESSIVE ENHANCEMENT)
42: 
43: | Situation | Level | Required Templates | Optional Templates |
44: |-----------|-------|-------------------|-------------------|
45: | Any task (baseline) | 1 | spec.md + plan.md + tasks.md + implementation-summary.md | resource-map.md (any level) |
46: | Needs QA validation | 2 | L1 + checklist.md | resource-map.md (any level) |
47: | Complex/architectural | 3 | L2 + decision-record.md | research/research.md, resource-map.md |
48: | Enterprise/governance heavy | 3+ | L3 file set rendered at Level 3+ | research/research.md, resource-map.md |
49: 
50: **LOC as soft guidance:**
51: - <100 LOC suggests Level 1
52: - 100-499 LOC suggests Level 2
53: - >=500 LOC suggests Level 3
54: - High complexity/risk and governance needs suggest Level 3+
55: 
56: ---
57: 
58: ## 3. TEMPLATE COPY COMMANDS (PROGRESSIVE)
59: 
60: ### Level 1: Baseline (ALL features start here)
61: 
62: ```bash
63: bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh --level 1 --path specs/###-name --name feature-name
64: ```
65: 
66: ### Level 2: Verification (complete set)
67: 
68: ```bash
69: bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh --level 2 --path specs/###-name --name feature-name
70: ```
71: 
72: ### Level 3: Full Documentation (complete set)
73: 
74: ```bash
75: bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh --level 3 --path specs/###-name --name feature-name
76: ```
77: 
78: ### Level 3+: Extended Documentation (complete set)
79: 
80: ```bash
81: bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh --level 3+ --path specs/###-name --name feature-name
82: ```
83: 
84: ### Optional Templates (Level 3 Only)
85: 
86: ```bash
87: ## Comprehensive Research (from root templates folder):
88: bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/templates/inline-gate-renderer.sh \
89:   --level 3 \
90:   --out-dir specs/###-name/research \
91:   .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/manifest/research.md.tmpl
92: ```
93: 
94: ---
95: 
96: ## 4. ESSENTIAL COMMANDS
97: 
98: ### Start Here: Command Map
99: 
100: If the runtime does not surface command menus clearly, use this compact command map first.
101: 
102: **SpecKit workflow commands**
103: - `/spec_kit:resume` - continue or recover existing spec-folder work
104: - `/spec_kit:plan` - define or refine a scoped spec and plan
105: - `/spec_kit:implement` - execute an existing spec and plan packet
106: - `/spec_kit:complete` - run the full spec-to-implementation lifecycle
107: - `/deep:research` - run iterative research before planning
108: - `/deep:review` - run iterative review and finding synthesis
109: 
110: **Memory commands**
111: - `/memory:save` - preserve the current session context into spec memory and refresh packet continuity
112: - `/memory:search` - retrieve prior context, decisions, and analysis
113: - `/memory:manage` - run maintenance, checkpoint, cleanup, and ingest operations
114: - `/memory:learn` - create or manage constitutional always-surface rules
115: 
116: **Nested changelog generator**
117: - `node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/spec-folder/nested-changelog.js <spec-folder> --write` - publish a packet-local changelog for a root spec or phase child
118: 
119: ### Find Next Spec Number
120: 
121: ```bash
122: ls -d specs/[0-9]*/ | sed 's/.*\/\([0-9]*\)-.*/\1/' | sort -n | tail -1
123: ```
124: 
125: Add 1 to the result to get your next number.
126: 
127: ### Create Spec Folder
128: 
129: ```bash
130: mkdir -p specs/###-short-name/
131: ```
132: 
133: ### Template Composition (Maintainer)
134: 
135: ```bash
136: # Render one or more manifest templates for inspection
137: bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/templates/inline-gate-renderer.sh \
138:   --level 3 \
139:   --out-dir /tmp/spec-kit-render \
140:   .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/manifest/spec.md.tmpl \
141:   .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/manifest/plan.md.tmpl
142: ```
143: 
144: `create.sh --path` validates the resolved target before writing and rejects traversal outside the repository. Post-create validation is opt-in: set `SPECKIT_POST_VALIDATE=1` when CI or a strict local workflow should run `validate.sh --quiet` immediately after scaffolding.
145: 
146: **Naming rules:**
147: - 2-3 words (shorter is better)
148: - Lowercase
149: - Hyphen-separated
150: - Action-noun structure
151: 
152: **Good examples:** `fix-typo`, `add-auth`, `mcp-code-mode`, `cli-codex`
153: 
154: ### Manual Context Save
155: 
156: Trigger manual context save:
157: ```
158: Say: "save context" or "save conversation"
159: ```
160: 
161: **Required Inputs:** Structured JSON is mandatory for routine saves, and the target spec folder is still required
162: ```
163: node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js /tmp/save-context-data-<session-id>.json specs/007-feature/
164: ```
165: 
166: ŌØī DO NOT use Write/Edit tools to author continuity surfaces directly.
167: 
168: ---
169: 
170: ## 5. PRE-IMPLEMENTATION CHECKLIST (PROGRESSIVE ENHANCEMENT)
171: 
172: Before making ANY file changes, verify:
173: 
174: - [ ] Determined level (1/2/3/3+) or exempt (typo fix)
175: - [ ] Created `/specs/[###-short-name]/`
176: - [ ] Scaffolded ALL REQUIRED templates for chosen level:
177:   - [ ] Level 1: spec.md + plan.md + tasks.md + implementation-summary.md
178:   - [ ] Level 2: Level 1 + checklist.md
179:   - [ ] Level 3: Level 2 + decision-record.md
180:   - [ ] Level 3+: Use full Level 3 file set rendered at Level 3+
181: - [ ] Rendered template output names are correct
182: - [ ] Filled ALL template sections with actual content
183: - [ ] Removed placeholder text and sample sections
184: - [ ] Copied optional templates if needed (Level 3 only)
185: - [ ] Presented approach to user (including templates used)
186: - [ ] Got explicit approval ("yes"/"go ahead"/"proceed")
187: 
188: **If ANY unchecked ŌåÆ STOP**
189: **If required template missing ŌåÆ Cannot claim completion**
190: 
191: ---
192: 
193: ## 6. FOLDER NAMING EXAMPLES
194: 
195: ### Good Examples Ō£ģ
196: 
197: - `fix-typo` (concise, clear)
198: - `add-validation` (action-noun)
199: - `implement-auth` (descriptive)
200: - `cdn-migration` (noun-noun acceptable)
201: - `hero-animation-v2` (version included)
202: 
203: ### Bad Examples ŌØī
204: 
205: - `fix-the-typo-in-header-component` (too long - max 4 words)
206: - `fixTypo` (not kebab-case)
207: - `fix_typo` (snake_case, should be kebab-case)
208: - `typo` (too vague, lacks context)
209: - `PROJ-123-fix` (no ticket numbers)
210: 
211: ---
212: 
213: ## 7. STATUS FIELD VALUES
214: 
215: | Status | Meaning | Reuse Priority |
216: |--------|---------|----------------|
217: | `draft` | Planning phase | 2 (can start) |
218: | `active` | Work in progress | 1 (highest - continue here) |
219: | `paused` | Temporarily on hold | 3 (can resume) |
220: | `complete` | Implementation finished | 4 (avoid reopening) |
221: | `archived` | Historical record | 5 (do not reuse) |
222: 
223: ---
224: 
225: ## 8. UPDATE VS CREATE DECISION
226: 
227: ### UPDATE Existing Spec When:
228: 
229: Ō£ģ Iterative development (continuing same feature)
230: Ō£ģ Bug fixes (fixing existing implementation)
231: Ō£ģ Scope escalation (work grew beyond estimate)
232: Ō£ģ Feature enhancement (adding to existing functionality)
233: Ō£ģ Resuming paused work
234: 
235: ### CREATE New Spec When:
236: 
237: ŌØī Distinct feature (completely separate)
238: ŌØī Different approach (alternative strategy)
239: ŌØī Separate user story (different requirement)
240: ŌØī Complete redesign (starting over)
241: ŌØī Unrelated work (no connection)
242: 
243: **Phased Packets**: If the target spec already has child phase folders,
244: resume/update in the active child phase - not the coordination root.
245: Use the root only for coordination snapshots, phase-map updates,
246: and tree-truth verification.
247: 
248: ---
249: 
250: ## 9. CONFIRMATION OPTIONS
251: 
252: When workflow prompts at conversation start:
253: 
254: **Option A:** Use detected folder (if related work found)
255: **Option B:** Create new spec folder with suggested number
256: **Option C:** Update one of the related specs shown
257: **Option D:** Skip spec folder creation (**WARNING:** Technical debt!)
258: **Option E:** Add phase to existing spec ŌĆö target a specific phase child (e.g., `specs/NNN-name/001-phase/`). Only shown when existing spec has high complexity or phased content.
259: 
260: **AI Agent Rule:** NEVER decide autonomously - ask user to choose (A/B/C/D/E)
261: 
262: ---
263: 
264: ## 10. LEVEL MIGRATION (PROGRESSIVE ENHANCEMENT)
265: 
266: ### Script-Assisted Upgrade (Recommended)
267: 
268: ```bash
269: # Upgrade to Level 2 (auto-detects current level)
270: bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/upgrade-level.sh specs/042-feature/ --to 2
271: 
272: # Upgrade to Level 3 (chains through intermediate levels)
273: bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/upgrade-level.sh specs/042-feature/ --to 3
274: 
275: # Upgrade to Level 3+
276: bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/upgrade-level.sh specs/042-feature/ --to 3+
277: 
278: # Preview changes first
279: bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/upgrade-level.sh specs/042-feature/ --to 3 --dry-run
280: ```
281: 
282: **Post-Upgrade:** After the script runs, AI **must** auto-populate all `<placeholder_token>` text in newly injected sections by reading existing spec context and deriving appropriate content.
283: 
284: Then verify placeholders are fully resolved:
285: 
286: ```bash
287: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/check-placeholders.sh specs/042-feature/
288: ```
289: 
290: ### Manual Fallback
291: 
292: | From | To | Files to Add |
293: |------|----|--------------|
294: | 1 ŌåÆ 2 | Add verification | checklist.md |
295: | 2 ŌåÆ 3 | Add decision documentation | decision-record.md (+ optional research/research.md) |
296: | 3 ŌåÆ 3+ | Add governance | Extended governance sections + AI protocols |
297: 
298: **Always:**
299: - Update `level:` field in metadata
300: - Add changelog entry noting escalation
301: - Keep existing documentation (progressive - don't delete)
302: - Inform user of level change
303: 
304: ---
305: 
306: ## 11. TROUBLESHOOTING
307: 
308: ### "I forgot to create the spec folder"
309: 
310: **Fix:**
311: 1. Stop coding immediately
312: 2. Create spec folder retroactively
313: 3. Document what was done and why
314: 4. Get user approval
315: 5. Continue with documentation in place
316: 
317: ---
318: 
319: ### "I'm not sure which level to choose"
320: 
321: **Solution:**
322: - When in doubt ŌåÆ choose **higher level**
323: - Ask user if confidence <80%
324: - Consider complexity and risk, not only LOC
325: - Better to over-document than under-document
326: 
327: ---
328: 
329: ### "Can I change levels mid-work?"
330: 
331: **Yes:**
332: - Going up: Add additional files (see Level Migration table)
333: - Going down: Keep existing docs (uncommon)
334: - Always: Inform user why level changed, update changelog
335: 
336: ---
337: 
338: ### "What if it's only exploration?"
339: 
340: **Rule:**
341: - Pure exploration/reading = NO spec needed
342: - Once you write/edit ANY files = SPEC REQUIRED
343: - If uncertain ŌåÆ create spec (safer)
344: 
345: ---
346: 
347: ### "Do I need specs for documentation changes?"
348: 
349: **YES - Documentation changes require specs the same as code changes.**
350: 
351: **Requires spec:**
352: - Ō£ģ Code files (*.js, *.ts, *.css, *.py)
353: - Ō£ģ Documentation files (*.md, *.txt, docs/)
354: - Ō£ģ Configuration files (*.json, *.yaml, *.toml)
355: - Ō£ģ Knowledge base files
356: - Ō£ģ Templates (.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/*.md)
357: - Ō£ģ Build files (package.json, requirements.txt)
358: 
359: **Exceptions (no spec needed):**
360: - ŌØī Single typo fix (<5 characters in one file)
361: - ŌØī Whitespace-only changes
362: - ŌØī Auto-generated updates (package-lock.json)
363: 
364: ---
365: 
366: ### "When do I need an Architecture Decision Record (ADR)?"
367: 
368: **Create `decision-record-*.md` when making:**
369: - Database, framework, or library choices
370: - Architectural pattern selections
371: - Major refactoring approaches
372: - Infrastructure/deployment strategy changes
373: 
374: **Format:** Use descriptive name (e.g., `decision-record-database-choice.md`)
375: 
376: ---
377: 
378: ## 12. WHAT REQUIRES SPEC FOLDERS
379: 
380: | File Type | Requires Spec | Examples |
381: |-----------|--------------|----------|
382: | Code files | Ō£ģ Yes | JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, CSS, HTML |
383: | Documentation | Ō£ģ Yes | Markdown, README updates, guides |
384: | Configuration | Ō£ģ Yes | JSON, YAML, TOML, .env templates |
385: | Knowledge base | Ō£ģ Yes | Project-specific knowledge files |
386: | Templates | Ō£ģ Yes | `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/*.md` modifications |
387: | Build/tooling | Ō£ģ Yes | package.json, requirements.txt, Dockerfile |
388: 
389: **Exceptions (no spec needed):**
390: - ŌØī Pure exploration/reading (no file modifications)
391: - ŌØī Single typo fixes (<5 characters in one file)
392: - ŌØī Whitespace-only changes
393: - ŌØī Auto-generated file updates (package-lock.json)
394: 
395: ---
396: 
397: ## 13. SKIP OPTION (OPTION D) USAGE
398: 
399: ### When Appropriate Ō£ģ
400: 
401: - Quick code exploration without implementation
402: - Testing a concept or approach
403: - Reading/analyzing existing code only
404: - Prototyping that will be discarded
405: 
406: ### When NOT Appropriate ŌØī
407: 
408: - Any actual implementation
409: - Bug fixes (even small ones)
410: - Feature work
411: - Refactoring
412: - Configuration changes
413: - Documentation updates
414: 
415: ### Technical Debt Warning
416: 
417: Skipping documentation:
418: - Makes future debugging harder
419: - Loses implementation decisions
420: - Breaks team handoffs
421: - Creates incomplete change history
422: 
423: **Use sparingly.** When in doubt, create spec folder (even minimal Level 1).
424: 
425: ---
426: 
427: ## 14. TEMPLATE ADAPTATION CHECKLIST (PROGRESSIVE ENHANCEMENT)
428: 
429: Before presenting documentation to user:
430: 
431: - [ ] All REQUIRED templates for level copied from `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/`:
432:   - [ ] Level 1: spec.md + plan.md + tasks.md + implementation-summary.md
433:   - [ ] Level 2: Level 1 + checklist.md
434:   - [ ] Level 3: Level 2 + decision-record.md
435: - [ ] Optional templates copied if needed (Level 3 only)
436: - [ ] All placeholders replaced (`<PLACEHOLDER>`, `<NEEDS_CLARIFICATION: ...>`)
437: - [ ] All sample content removed (`<!-- SAMPLE CONTENT -->`)
438: - [ ] Template footer deleted
439: - [ ] Metadata block filled correctly
440: - [ ] All sections filled with actual content (or marked "N/A")
441: - [ ] Cross-references to sibling documents working
442: - [ ] Numbering and emojis preserved
443: - [ ] Structure matches template
444: - [ ] Descriptive filenames used (for decision records)
445: 
446: **If ANY unchecked ŌåÆ Fix before user presentation**
447: **If required template missing ŌåÆ Cannot claim completion**
448: 
449: ---
450: 
451: ## 15. CONTEXT SAVE & HANDOVER
452: 
453: ### Context Save
454: 
455: **Manual triggers (OpenCode):**
456: - Command: `/memory:save`
457: - Keywords: "save context", "save conversation", "save this"
458: 
459: **Note:** OpenCode does not support automatic interval-based saves (hooks are event-triggered via plugins, not time-interval).
460: 
461: **MANDATORY:** Use generate-context.js for memory save:
462: ```
463: node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js /tmp/save-context-data-<session-id>.json specs/###-folder/
464: ```
465: 
466: ŌØī DO NOT use Write/Edit tools to author continuity surfaces directly.
467: 
468: > **Recovery Integration:** Packet recovery is canonical-first. Start from `handover.md`, then `_memory.continuity` in `implementation-summary.md`, then the rest of the packet docs before widening into supporting indexed retrieval.
469: 
470: **Phased Resume**: When the target folder has child phases, first resolve
471: whether you need the coordination root or an active child.
472: Load/save memory in the child packet by default.
473: Only load root memory when updating coordination snapshots.
474: 
475: **Phase-Parent Resume Ladder**: When the resume target is a phase parent (folder has `[0-9]{3}-name/` children with `spec.md` or `description.json`), `/spec_kit:resume` honors the pointer first:
476: 
477: 1. Read `derived.last_active_child_id` from the parent's `graph-metadata.json`. If non-null AND `derived.last_active_at` parses as ISO-8601 within the last 24 hours, recurse directly into that child and apply the child's normal handover ŌåÆ `_memory.continuity` ŌåÆ spec docs ladder.
478: 2. If the pointer is null, missing, malformed, or older than 24 hours, fall back to listing children with statuses (sourced from each child's `graph-metadata.json` `derived.status`) and let the user pick.
479: 3. `--no-redirect` flag bypasses the pointer step entirely and shows the parent's spec.md plus child list.
480: 
481: The pointer is maintained automatically by the generator: parent-level saves write `last_active_child_id = null`; child saves bubble up the child's `packet_id`. Atomic write via temp+rename. No `_memory.continuity` block exists at a phase parent (per the lean trio policy ŌĆö parents have no `implementation-summary.md`).
482: 
483: **Save location:**
484: - Primary: the active packet's canonical continuity surfaces
485: - Core continuity block: `_memory.continuity` in `implementation-summary.md`
486: - No workspace-root fallback; direct CLI targets stay inside the selected root spec or phase packet
487: 
488: ### Session Continuity Save
489: 
490: **Command:** `/memory:save`
491: 
492: **Purpose:** Refresh packet continuity before a pause or handoff while keeping `handover.md`, `_memory.continuity`, and the packet docs aligned for the next `/spec_kit:resume` pass.
493: 
494: **Use when:**
495: - Ending a long session
496: - Context compaction detected
497: - Handing off to another session
498: - Before complex multi-step operations
499: 
500: ---
501: 
502: ## 16. PHASE WORKFLOW SHORTCUTS
503: 
504: ### Phase Commands
505: 
506: | Command | Description |
507: |---------|-------------|
508: | `/spec_kit:plan :with-phases` | Trigger phase decomposition as pre-workflow in plan or complete |
509: | `create.sh --phase --phases N --phase-names a,b,c <description>` | Create a phase parent and named child phase folders |
510: | `create.sh --phase --parent <parent> --phases N --phase-names a,b <description>` | Add named child phases to an existing parent spec |
511: | `validate.sh <parent> --recursive` | Validate parent and all child phase folders |
512: | `node .../nested-changelog.js <spec-folder> --write` | Write a packet-local root or phase changelog into the parent packet `changelog/` folder |
513: 
514: ### Phase Quick Reference
515: 
516: **Detection:** Complexity score >= 25 AND level >= 3
517: 
518: **Scoring dimensions:** Architectural (10) + Files>15 (10) + LOC>800 (10) + Risk>=2 (10) + Extreme scale (10)
519: 
520: **Suggested phase counts:**
521: - Score 25-34: 2 phases
522: - Score 35-44: 3 phases
523: - Score 45+: 4 phases
524: 
525: ### Phase Folder Pattern
526: 
527: ```
528: specs/###-parent/
529: Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ spec.md           # Phase Documentation Map
530: Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ changelog/        # Root + phase changelog artifacts
531: Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ 001-phase-one/    # Independent child spec folder
532: Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ 002-phase-two/
533: ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ 003-phase-three/
534: ```
535: 
536: ### Phase vs Version Sub-Folders
537: 
538: - **Phases:** Parallel decomposition of different work streams
539: - **Versions:** Sequential iterations of the same work
540: 
541: **Full documentation:** See [phase_definitions.md](../structure/phase_definitions.md)
542: 
543: ---
544: 
545: ## 17. AGENT CRITICAL RULES (PROGRESSIVE ENHANCEMENT)
546: 
547: ### Absolutely Required
548: 
549: - **NEVER create documentation from scratch** - Always copy from templates
550: - **ALWAYS copy from `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/`** directory
551: - **ALWAYS copy ALL REQUIRED templates for chosen level**:
552:   - Level 1: spec.md + plan.md + tasks.md + implementation-summary.md
553:   - Level 2: Level 1 + checklist.md
554:   - Level 3: Level 2 + decision-record.md
555: - **ALWAYS fill ALL placeholders** - No `<PLACEHOLDER>` marker in final docs
556: - **ALWAYS respond to workflow prompts** - Ask user for A/B/C/D/E choice
557: - **ALWAYS get user approval** - Explicit "yes" before file changes
558: - **Use distributed governance for spec docs** ŌĆö Any agent writing packet markdown must use the level templates, run `validate.sh --strict`, and route continuity refreshes through `/memory:save`. Preserve `@deep-research` ownership of `research/research.md` and `@debug` ownership of `debug-delegation.md`.
559: 
560: ### Enforcement
561: 
562: - **Verify required templates exist** before claiming completion
563: - **LOC thresholds are soft guidance** - use judgment
564: - **Enforcement is manual** - verify before claiming done
565: 
566: ### Applies to ALL
567: 
568: - Code files (*.js, *.ts, *.py, *.css, *.html)
569: - Documentation files (*.md, README, docs/)
570: - Configuration files (*.json, *.yaml, *.toml)
571: - Knowledge base files (project-specific)
572: - Template files (.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/*.md)
573: - Build files (package.json, requirements.txt)
574: 
575: **No exceptions** (unless user explicitly selects Option D)
576: 
577: ---
578: 
579: ## 18. CHECKLIST VERIFICATION PROTOCOL (LEVEL 2+)
580: 
581: ### When to Use
582: 
583: Checklist verification is **MANDATORY** for all Level 2+ documentation:
584: - Level 2: Features requiring QA validation (100-499 LOC guidance)
585: - Level 3: Complex/architectural work (>=500 LOC guidance)
586: - Level 3+: Complex governance/multi-agent work (high complexity/risk)
587: 
588: The `checklist.md` is an **ACTIVE VERIFICATION TOOL**, not passive documentation.
589: 
590: ### Verification Process
591: 
592: ```
593: CHECKLIST AS VERIFICATION TOOL (Level 2+):
594: 1. LOAD checklist.md at completion phase
595: 2. VERIFY each item systematically (P0 first, then P1, then P2)
596: 3. MARK items [x] with evidence (links, test results, etc.)
597: 4. BLOCK completion until all P0/P1 items verified
598: 5. DOCUMENT any deferred P2 items with reason
599: ```
600: 
601: ### Priority Levels
602: 
603: | Priority | Meaning | Completion Gate |
604: |----------|---------|-----------------|
605: | **P0** | Blocker | MUST pass - work is incomplete without this |
606: | **P1** | Required | MUST pass - required for production readiness |
607: | **P2** | Optional | Can defer with documented reason |
608: 
609: ### How to Verify Items
610: 
611: For each checklist item:
612: 
613: 1. **Perform the check** (run test, inspect code, verify behavior)
614: 2. **Mark with [x]** when verified
615: 3. **Add evidence** (link, test output, screenshot reference)
616: 4. **Add timestamp** for audit trail
617: 
618: ### Example: Properly Verified Checklist
619: 
620: ```markdown
621: ## Verification Checklist
622: 
623: ### P0 - Blockers (MUST pass)
624: - [x] Core functionality works as specified
625:   - Evidence: Manual test passed - modal opens/closes correctly
626:   - Verified: 2025-12-01 14:30
627: - [x] No console errors in browser
628:   - Evidence: DevTools console clean on Chrome/Firefox/Safari
629:   - Verified: 2025-12-01 14:32
630: 
631: ### P1 - Required (MUST pass for production)
632: - [x] Responsive design verified (mobile/tablet/desktop)
633:   - Evidence: Tested at 375px, 768px, 1440px breakpoints
634:   - Verified: 2025-12-01 14:35
635: - [x] Accessibility: keyboard navigation works
636:   - Evidence: Tab/Enter/Escape all functional
637:   - Verified: 2025-12-01 14:38
638: 
639: ### P2 - Optional (can defer)
640: - [ ] Performance optimization for large datasets
641:   - Deferred: Not needed for MVP, tracked in issue #123
642: - [x] Animation smoothness verified
643:   - Evidence: 60fps confirmed in Performance tab
644:   - Verified: 2025-12-01 14:40
645: ```
646: 
647: ### AI Agent Rules
648: 
649: - **NEVER claim completion without running checklist verification**
650: - **ALWAYS load checklist.md before stating work is done**
651: - **ALWAYS mark items with evidence, not only [x]**
652: - **ALWAYS complete all P0/P1 before claiming done**
653: - **ALWAYS document why P2 items are deferred (if applicable)**
654: 
655: ---
656: 
657: ## 19. CORE PRINCIPLE
658: 
659: **Every file change deserves documentation.**
660: 
661: Future you will thank present you for creating that spec folder.
662: 
663: When in doubt:
664: - Document more rather than less
665: - Choose higher level over lower
666: - Create spec folder over skipping
667: - Ask user rather than guessing
668: 
669: **Cost of creating spec << Cost of reconstructing lost context later**
670: 
671: ---
672: 
673: ## 20. RELATED RESOURCES
674: 
675: ### Reference Files
676: - [template_guide.md](../templates/template_guide.md) - Template selection, adaptation, and quality standards
677: - level specifications reference - Complete Level 1-3 requirements and migration
678: - [path_scoped_rules.md](../validation/path_scoped_rules.md) - Path-scoped validation rules reference
679: 
680: ### Templates (Organized by Level)

# Quick Reference - Commands, Checklists & Troubleshooting

Fast lookup for spec folder commands, checklists, and troubleshooting.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Quick reference for spec folder management, commands, and troubleshooting using the progressive enhancement model.

### Progressive Enhancement Model

```
Level 1 (Baseline):     spec.md + plan.md + tasks.md + implementation-summary.md
Level 2 (Verification): Level 1 + checklist.md
Level 3 (Full):         Level 2 + decision-record.md + optional research
Level 3+ (Extended):    Level 3 + governance/AI execution content
```

### Key Points

- LOC thresholds are **SOFT GUIDANCE** (not enforcement)
- **Enforcement is MANUAL** - verify required templates exist before claiming completion

---

## 2. LEVEL DECISION SHORTCUTS (PROGRESSIVE ENHANCEMENT)

| Situation | Level | Required Templates | Optional Templates |
|-----------|-------|-------------------|-------------------|
| Any task (baseline) | 1 | spec.md + plan.md + tasks.md + implementation-summary.md | resource-map.md (any level) |
| Needs QA validation | 2 | L1 + checklist.md | resource-map.md (any level) |
| Complex/architectural | 3 | L2 + decision-record.md | research/research.md, resource-map.md |
| Enterprise/governance heavy | 3+ | L3 file set rendered at Level 3+ | research/research.md, resource-map.md |

**LOC as soft guidance:**
- <100 LOC suggests Level 1
- 100-499 LOC suggests Level 2
- >=500 LOC suggests Level 3
- High complexity/risk and governance needs suggest Level 3+

---

## 3. TEMPLATE COPY COMMANDS (PROGRESSIVE)

### Level 1: Baseline (ALL features start here)

```bash
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh --level 1 --path specs/###-name --name feature-name
```

### Level 2: Verification (complete set)

```bash
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh --level 2 --path specs/###-name --name feature-name
```

### Level 3: Full Documentation (complete set)

```bash
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh --level 3 --path specs/###-name --name feature-name
```

### Level 3+: Extended Documentation (complete set)

```bash
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh --level 3+ --path specs/###-name --name feature-name
```

### Optional Templates (Level 3 Only)

```bash
## Comprehensive Research (from root templates folder):
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/templates/inline-gate-renderer.sh \
  --level 3 \
  --out-dir specs/###-name/research \
  .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/manifest/research.md.tmpl
```

---

## 4. ESSENTIAL COMMANDS

### Start Here: Command Map

If the runtime does not surface command menus clearly, use this compact command map first.

**SpecKit workflow commands**
- `/spec_kit:resume` - continue or recover existing spec-folder work
- `/spec_kit:plan` - define or refine a scoped spec and plan
- `/spec_kit:implement` - execute an existing spec and plan packet
- `/spec_kit:complete` - run the full spec-to-implementation lifecycle
- `/deep:research` - run iterative research before planning
- `/deep:review` - run iterative review and finding synthesis

**Memory commands**
- `/memory:save` - preserve the current session context into spec memory and refresh packet continuity
- `/memory:search` - retrieve prior context, decisions, and analysis
- `/memory:manage` - run maintenance, checkpoint, cleanup, and ingest operations
- `/memory:learn` - create or manage constitutional always-surface rules

**Nested changelog generator**
- `node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/spec-folder/nested-changelog.js <spec-folder> --write` - publish a packet-local changelog for a root spec or phase child

### Find Next Spec Number

```bash
ls -d specs/[0-9]*/ | sed 's/.*\/\([0-9]*\)-.*/\1/' | sort -n | tail -1
```

Add 1 to the result to get your next number.

### Create Spec Folder

```bash
mkdir -p specs/###-short-name/
```

### Template Composition (Maintainer)

```bash
# Render one or more manifest templates for inspection
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/templates/inline-gate-renderer.sh \
  --level 3 \
  --out-dir /tmp/spec-kit-render \
  .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/manifest/spec.md.tmpl \
  .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/manifest/plan.md.tmpl
```

`create.sh --path` validates the resolved target before writing and rejects traversal outside the repository. Post-create validation is opt-in: set `SPECKIT_POST_VALIDATE=1` when CI or a strict local workflow should run `validate.sh --quiet` immediately after scaffolding.

**Naming rules:**
- 2-3 words (shorter is better)
- Lowercase
- Hyphen-separated
- Action-noun structure

**Good examples:** `fix-typo`, `add-auth`, `mcp-code-mode`, `cli-codex`

### Manual Context Save

Trigger manual context save:
```
Say: "save context" or "save conversation"
```

**Required Inputs:** Structured JSON is mandatory for routine saves, and the target spec folder is still required
```
node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js /tmp/save-context-data-<session-id>.json specs/007-feature/
```

ŌØī DO NOT use Write/Edit tools to author continuity surfaces directly.

---

## 5. PRE-IMPLEMENTATION CHECKLIST (PROGRESSIVE ENHANCEMENT)

Before making ANY file changes, verify:

- [ ] Determined level (1/2/3/3+) or exempt (typo fix)
- [ ] Created `/specs/[###-short-name]/`
- [ ] Scaffolded ALL REQUIRED templates for chosen level:
  - [ ] Level 1: spec.md + plan.md + tasks.md + implementation-summary.md
  - [ ] Level 2: Level 1 + checklist.md
  - [ ] Level 3: Level 2 + decision-record.md
  - [ ] Level 3+: Use full Level 3 file set rendered at Level 3+
- [ ] Rendered template output names are correct
- [ ] Filled ALL template sections with actual content
- [ ] Removed placeholder text and sample sections
- [ ] Copied optional templates if needed (Level 3 only)
- [ ] Presented approach to user (including templates used)
- [ ] Got explicit approval ("yes"/"go ahead"/"proceed")

**If ANY unchecked ŌåÆ STOP**
**If required template missing ŌåÆ Cannot claim completion**

---

## 6. FOLDER NAMING EXAMPLES

### Good Examples Ō£ģ

- `fix-typo` (concise, clear)
- `add-validation` (action-noun)
- `implement-auth` (descriptive)
- `cdn-migration` (noun-noun acceptable)
- `hero-animation-v2` (version included)

### Bad Examples ŌØī

- `fix-the-typo-in-header-component` (too long - max 4 words)
- `fixTypo` (not kebab-case)
- `fix_typo` (snake_case, should be kebab-case)
- `typo` (too vague, lacks context)
- `PROJ-123-fix` (no ticket numbers)

---

## 7. STATUS FIELD VALUES

| Status | Meaning | Reuse Priority |
|--------|---------|----------------|
| `draft` | Planning phase | 2 (can start) |
| `active` | Work in progress | 1 (highest - continue here) |
| `paused` | Temporarily on hold | 3 (can resume) |
| `complete` | Implementation finished | 4 (avoid reopening) |
| `archived` | Historical record | 5 (do not reuse) |

---

## 8. UPDATE VS CREATE DECISION

### UPDATE Existing Spec When:

Ō£ģ Iterative development (continuing same feature)
Ō£ģ Bug fixes (fixing existing implementation)
Ō£ģ Scope escalation (work grew beyond estimate)
Ō£ģ Feature enhancement (adding to existing functionality)
Ō£ģ Resuming paused work

### CREATE New Spec When:

ŌØī Distinct feature (completely separate)
ŌØī Different approach (alternative strategy)
ŌØī Separate user story (different requirement)
ŌØī Complete redesign (starting over)
ŌØī Unrelated work (no connection)

**Phased Packets**: If the target spec already has child phase folders,
resume/update in the active child phase - not the coordination root.
Use the root only for coordination snapshots, phase-map updates,
and tree-truth verification.

## 9. CONFIRMATION OPTIONS

When workflow prompts at conversation start:

**Option A:** Use detected folder (if related work found)
**Option B:** Create new spec folder with suggested number
**Option C:** Update one of the related specs shown
**Option D:** Skip spec folder creation (**WARNING:** Technical debt!)
**Option E:** Add phase to existing spec ŌĆö target a specific phase child (e.g., `specs/NNN-name/001-phase/`). Only shown when existing spec has high complexity or phased content.

**AI Agent Rule:** NEVER decide autonomously - ask user to choose (A/B/C/D/E)

## 11. TROUBLESHOOTING

### "I forgot to create the spec folder"

**Fix:**
1. Stop coding immediately
2. Create spec folder retroactively
3. Document what was done and why
4. Get user approval
5. Continue with documentation in place

---

### "I'm not sure which level to choose"

**Solution:**
- When in doubt ŌåÆ choose **higher level**
- Ask user if confidence <80%
- Consider complexity and risk, not only LOC
- Better to over-document than under-document

---

### "Can I change levels mid-work?"

**Yes:**
- Going up: Add additional files (see Level Migration table)
- Going down: Keep existing docs (uncommon)
- Always: Inform user why level changed, update changelog

---

### "What if it's only exploration?"

**Rule:**
- Pure exploration/reading = NO spec needed
- Once you write/edit ANY files = SPEC REQUIRED
- If uncertain ŌåÆ create spec (safer)

---

### "Do I need specs for documentation changes?"

**YES - Documentation changes require specs the same as code changes.**

**Requires spec:**
- Ō£ģ Code files (*.js, *.ts, *.css, *.py)
- Ō£ģ Documentation files (*.md, *.txt, docs/)
- Ō£ģ Configuration files (*.json, *.yaml, *.toml)
- Ō£ģ Knowledge base files
- Ō£ģ Templates (.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/*.md)
- Ō£ģ Build files (package.json, requirements.txt)

**Exceptions (no spec needed):**
- ŌØī Single typo fix (<5 characters in one file)
- ŌØī Whitespace-only changes
- ŌØī Auto-generated updates (package-lock.json)

---

### "When do I need an Architecture Decision Record (ADR)?"

**Create `decision-record-*.md` when making:**
- Database, framework, or library choices
- Architectural pattern selections
- Major refactoring approaches
- Infrastructure/deployment strategy changes

**Format:** Use descriptive name (e.g., `decision-record-database-choice.md`)

## 15. CONTEXT SAVE & HANDOVER

### Context Save

**Manual triggers (OpenCode):**
- Command: `/memory:save`
- Keywords: "save context", "save conversation", "save this"

**Note:** OpenCode does not support automatic interval-based saves (hooks are event-triggered via plugins, not time-interval).

**MANDATORY:** Use generate-context.js for memory save:
```
node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js /tmp/save-context-data-<session-id>.json specs/###-folder/
```

ŌØī DO NOT use Write/Edit tools to author continuity surfaces directly.

> **Recovery Integration:** Packet recovery is canonical-first. Start from `handover.md`, then `_memory.continuity` in `implementation-summary.md`, then the rest of the packet docs before widening into supporting indexed retrieval.

**Phased Resume**: When the target folder has child phases, first resolve
whether you need the coordination root or an active child.
Load/save memory in the child packet by default.
Only load root memory when updating coordination snapshots.

**Phase-Parent Resume Ladder**: When the resume target is a phase parent (folder has `[0-9]{3}-name/` children with `spec.md` or `description.json`), `/spec_kit:resume` honors the pointer first:

1. Read `derived.last_active_child_id` from the parent's `graph-metadata.json`. If non-null AND `derived.last_active_at` parses as ISO-8601 within the last 24 hours, recurse directly into that child and apply the child's normal handover ŌåÆ `_memory.continuity` ŌåÆ spec docs ladder.
2. If the pointer is null, missing, malformed, or older than 24 hours, fall back to listing children with statuses (sourced from each child's `graph-metadata.json` `derived.status`) and let the user pick.
3. `--no-redirect` flag bypasses the pointer step entirely and shows the parent's spec.md plus child list.

The pointer is maintained automatically by the generator: parent-level saves write `last_active_child_id = null`; child saves bubble up the child's `packet_id`. Atomic write via temp+rename. No `_memory.continuity` block exists at a phase parent (per the lean trio policy ŌĆö parents have no `implementation-summary.md`).

**Save location:**
- Primary: the active packet's canonical continuity surfaces
- Core continuity block: `_memory.continuity` in `implementation-summary.md`
- No workspace-root fallback; direct CLI targets stay inside the selected root spec or phase packet

### Session Continuity Save

**Command:** `/memory:save`

**Purpose:** Refresh packet continuity before a pause or handoff while keeping `handover.md`, `_memory.continuity`, and the packet docs aligned for the next `/spec_kit:resume` pass.

**Use when:**
- Ending a long session
- Context compaction detected
- Handing off to another session
- Before complex multi-step operations

## 16. PHASE WORKFLOW SHORTCUTS

### Phase Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/spec_kit:plan :with-phases` | Trigger phase decomposition as pre-workflow in plan or complete |
| `create.sh --phase --phases N --phase-names a,b,c <description>` | Create a phase parent and named child phase folders |
| `create.sh --phase --parent <parent> --phases N --phase-names a,b <description>` | Add named child phases to an existing parent spec |
| `validate.sh <parent> --recursive` | Validate parent and all child phase folders |
| `node .../nested-changelog.js <spec-folder> --write` | Write a packet-local root or phase changelog into the parent packet `changelog/` folder |

### Phase Quick Reference

**Detection:** Complexity score >= 25 AND level >= 3

**Scoring dimensions:** Architectural (10) + Files>15 (10) + LOC>800 (10) + Risk>=2 (10) + Extreme scale (10)

**Suggested phase counts:**
- Score 25-34: 2 phases
- Score 35-44: 3 phases
- Score 45+: 4 phases

### Phase Folder Pattern

```
specs/###-parent/
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ spec.md           # Phase Documentation Map
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ changelog/        # Root + phase changelog artifacts
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ 001-phase-one/    # Independent child spec folder
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ 002-phase-two/
ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ 003-phase-three/
```

### Phase vs Version Sub-Folders

- **Phases:** Parallel decomposition of different work streams
- **Versions:** Sequential iterations of the same work

**Full documentation:** See [phase_definitions.md](../structure/phase_definitions.md)

## 18. CHECKLIST VERIFICATION PROTOCOL (LEVEL 2+)

### When to Use

Checklist verification is **MANDATORY** for all Level 2+ documentation:
- Level 2: Features requiring QA validation (100-499 LOC guidance)
- Level 3: Complex/architectural work (>=500 LOC guidance)
- Level 3+: Complex governance/multi-agent work (high complexity/risk)

The `checklist.md` is an **ACTIVE VERIFICATION TOOL**, not passive documentation.

### Verification Process

```
CHECKLIST AS VERIFICATION TOOL (Level 2+):
1. LOAD checklist.md at completion phase
2. VERIFY each item systematically (P0 first, then P1, then P2)
3. MARK items [x] with evidence (links, test results, etc.)
4. BLOCK completion until all P0/P1 items verified
5. DOCUMENT any deferred P2 items with reason
```

### Priority Levels

| Priority | Meaning | Completion Gate |
|----------|---------|-----------------|
| **P0** | Blocker | MUST pass - work is incomplete without this |
| **P1** | Required | MUST pass - required for production readiness |
| **P2** | Optional | Can defer with documented reason |

### How to Verify Items

For each checklist item:

1. **Perform the check** (run test, inspect code, verify behavior)
2. **Mark with [x]** when verified
3. **Add evidence** (link, test output, screenshot reference)
4. **Add timestamp** for audit trail

### Example: Properly Verified Checklist

```markdown
## Verification Checklist

### P0 - Blockers (MUST pass)
- [x] Core functionality works as specified
  - Evidence: Manual test passed - modal opens/closes correctly
  - Verified: 2025-12-01 14:30
- [x] No console errors in browser
  - Evidence: DevTools console clean on Chrome/Firefox/Safari
  - Verified: 2025-12-01 14:32

### P1 - Required (MUST pass for production)
- [x] Responsive design verified (mobile/tablet/desktop)
  - Evidence: Tested at 375px, 768px, 1440px breakpoints
  - Verified: 2025-12-01 14:35
- [x] Accessibility: keyboard navigation works
  - Evidence: Tab/Enter/Escape all functional
  - Verified: 2025-12-01 14:38

### P2 - Optional (can defer)
- [ ] Performance optimization for large datasets
  - Deferred: Not needed for MVP, tracked in issue #123
- [x] Animation smoothness verified
  - Evidence: 60fps confirmed in Performance tab
  - Verified: 2025-12-01 14:40
```

### AI Agent Rules

- **NEVER claim completion without running checklist verification**
- **ALWAYS load checklist.md before stating work is done**
- **ALWAYS mark items with evidence, not only [x]**
- **ALWAYS complete all P0/P1 before claiming done**
- **ALWAYS document why P2 items are deferred (if applicable)**

Future you will thank present you for creating that spec folder.

**Cost of creating spec << Cost of reconstructing lost context later**

14: # Memory System Reference - MCP Tools & Behavior
15: 
16: Spec Kit Memory MCP tools, behavior notes, and configuration options.
17: 
18: ---
19: 
20: ## 1. OVERVIEW
21: 
22: Current baseline: schema v37 (`document_type`, `spec_level`, trigger embeddings, provenance `source_kind`, idempotency receipts, near-duplicate hints and tombstone partitions), 2 active indexed content sources plus retired compatibility artifacts, 7 intent types, and `includeSpecDocs: true` by default.
23: 
24: The Spec Kit Memory system provides context preservation across sessions through vector-based semantic search and packet-first continuity. Phase 018 makes `handover.md -> _memory.continuity -> spec docs` the canonical recovery chain; retired `[spec]/memory/*.md` artifacts are no longer produced at save time and only matter when older packets still contain them. This reference covers MCP tool behavior, importance tiers, decay scoring, and configuration.
25: 
26: When a save mutates indexed state, the runtime also updates the `DB_UPDATED_FILE` marker from `mcp_server/Level sourceconfig.ts`. Long-lived MCP processes poll that marker with `checkDatabaseUpdated()` so canonical-doc writes become visible without restarting the server.
27: 
28: ### Architecture
29: 
30: | Component | Location | Purpose |
31: |-----------|----------|---------|
32: | MCP Server | `mcp_server/context-server.ts` | Spec Kit Memory MCP with vector search |
33: | CLI Shim | `.opencode/bin/spec-memory.cjs` | Daemon-backed CLI over the same tool surface; additive warm-only fallback when runtime MCP transport is unavailable (exit `75` = retryable daemon/IPC unavailability) |
34: | Database | `mcp_server/database/context-index__*.sqlite` | SQLite with FTS5 + vector embeddings; active profile filename resolved by `shared/embeddings/profile.ts:resolveActiveProfileDbPath` |
35: | Constitutional | `constitutional/` | Always-surface rules (Gate 3 enforcement) |
36: | Scripts | runtime `scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js` (source: `scripts/memory/generate-context.ts`) | Canonical continuity save entrypoint for packet docs |
37: 
38: ### Core Support
39: 
40: - **Semantic search** - Find indexed spec-doc records by meaning, not just keywords
41: - **Importance tiers** - Six-level system for prioritizing context
42: - **Decay scoring** - Recent indexed spec-doc records rank higher than older ones
43: - **Checkpoints** - Save/restore indexed-continuity state snapshots
44: - **Constitutional tier** - Critical rules that always surface
45: 
46: ### Indexable Content Sources
47: 
48: The indexed-continuity store indexes content from two active sources, plus a retired-compatibility row preserved for read-side retrieval against historical packets:
49: 
50: | Source | Location Pattern | Memory Type | Default Tier | Discovery |
51: |--------|-----------------|-------------|--------------|-----------|
52: | **Spec Documents** | `specs/**/*.md` and `<active-spec-folder>/**/*.md` | Per-type (spec, plan, tasks, etc.) | `normal` | `findSpecDocuments()` |
53: | **Constitutional Rules** | `.opencode/skills/*/constitutional/*.md` | `meta-cognitive` | `constitutional` | `findConstitutionalFiles()` |
54: | **Retired Compatibility Artifacts** | Older `specs/*/memory/*.{md,txt}` files already present in historical packets | Varies (episodic, procedural, etc.) | `normal` | Historical compatibility only |
55: 
56: **Content Source Behavior:**
57: 
58: - **Spec Documents** ŌĆö Canonical packet continuity source. Recovery should read `handover.md`, then `_memory.continuity`, then the rest of the packet docs before widening into search.
59: - **Constitutional Rules** ŌĆö Always-surface critical rules. Injected at top of every search result. No decay.
60: - **Retired Compatibility Artifacts** ŌĆö Older session notes may still exist in historical packets, but save workflows no longer produce them and operators should not treat them as an active surface.
61: 
62: **Spec Document Indexing Pipeline:**
63: 1. `findSpecDocuments()` walks both supported specs roots and discovers supported doc filenames
64: 2. `isMemoryFile()` validates each document as an indexable file
65: 3. `extractDocumentType()` infers `document_type` (spec/plan/tasks/etc.) for scoring
66: 4. Documents are indexed alongside memory and constitutional files
67: 5. `createSpecDocumentChain()` links lifecycle docs for causal traversal
68: 
69: ---
70: 
71: ## 2. IMPORTANCE TIERS
72: 
73: Six-tier system for prioritizing memory relevance:
74: 
75: | Tier | Weight | searchBoost | Purpose | Auto-Surface |
76: |------|--------|-------------|---------|--------------|
77: | **Constitutional** | 1.0 | 3.0 | Critical rules that ALWAYS apply | Yes (top of every search) |
78: | **Critical** | 1.0 | 2.0 | High-importance context | Yes (high relevance) |
79: | **Important** | 0.8 | 1.5 | Significant decisions/context | Relevance-based |
80: | **Normal** | 0.5 | 1.0 | Standard session context | Relevance-based |
81: | **Temporary** | 0.3 | 0.5 | Short-term notes | Relevance-based |
82: | **Deprecated** | 0.1 | 0.0 | Outdated (kept for history) | Never (excluded from search) |
83: 
84: **searchBoost Multipliers:** Applied to search scores to prioritize higher tiers:
85: - Constitutional memories get 3x boost in search ranking
86: - Critical memories get 2x boost
87: - Important memories get 1.5x boost
88: - Normal memories have no boost (1.0x)
89: - Temporary and Deprecated get reduced visibility (0.5x and 0x respectively)
90: 
91: ### Constitutional Tier Behavior
92: 
93: - Stored in `constitutional/` folder
94: - Auto-indexed on MCP server startup
95: - **ALWAYS** surface at top of search results, regardless of query
96: - Used for gate enforcement (e.g., "always ask spec folder question")
97: - EXEMPT from decay scoring (always max relevance)
98: 
99: ---

147: | L7: Maintenance | `embedder_set()` | Change the active embedder profile | Controlled embedder swap |
148: | L7: Maintenance | `embedder_status()` | Report active embedder health and profile | Diagnose embedding readiness |
149: | L7: Maintenance | `session_health()` | Report session priming and freshness status | Detect context drift during long sessions |
150: | L7: Maintenance | `session_resume()` | Resume memory and structural readiness context | Detailed recovery payload after reconnect |
151: | L7: Maintenance | `session_bootstrap()` | Composite bootstrap for resume and health checks | Fresh-session readiness bundle |
152: 
153: Code-graph implementation and package docs are owned by `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/`; use `mk-code-index` for structural `code_graph_*` tools.
154: 
155: ### memory_index_scan() Parameters
156: 
157: | Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
158: |-----------|------|---------|-------------|
159: | `specFolder` | string | - | Limit scan to specific spec folder |
160: | `force` | boolean | false | Force re-index all files (ignore content hash) |
161: | `includeConstitutional` | boolean | true | Scan `.opencode/skills/*/constitutional/` directories |
162: | `includeSpecDocs` | boolean | true | Scan for spec folder documents in `.opencode/specs/`. When true, discovers and indexes specs, plans, tasks, decision records, etc. with document-type scoring multipliers (11 types). Also controllable via `SPECKIT_INDEX_SPEC_DOCS` env var. |
163: | `incremental` | boolean | true | Skip files whose mtime and content hash are unchanged since last index |
164: 
165: ---
166: 
167: ## 4. MEMORY_SEARCH() BEHAVIOR
168: 
169: ### Parameter Requirements
170: 
171: > **IMPORTANT:** You MUST provide either `query` OR `concepts` parameter. Calling `memory_search({ specFolder: "..." })` without a search parameter will cause an E040 error.
172: 
173: **Required Parameters (one of):**
174: - `query`: Natural language search query (string)
175: - `concepts`: Multiple concepts for AND search (array of 2-5 strings)
176: 
177: **Optional Parameters:**
178: - `specFolder`: Limit search to specific spec folder
179: - `tenantId` (string, optional): Tenant boundary for governed retrieval
180: - `userId` (string, optional): User boundary for governed retrieval
181: - `agentId` (string, optional): Agent boundary for governed retrieval
182: - `includeContent`: Include full file content in results
183: - `includeConstitutional`: Include constitutional tier memories
184: - `anchors`: Array of anchor IDs for targeted section retrieval (token-efficient)
185: - `tier`: Filter by importance tier
186: - `limit`: Maximum results to return
187: - `useDecay`: Apply temporal decay scoring
188: 
189: When operating in shared or multi-actor deployments, always provide scope parameters to prevent cross-scope data exposure.
190: 
191: ### Parameter Reference
192: 
193: | Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
194: |-----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
195: | `query` | string | One of query/concepts | - | Natural language search query |
196: | `concepts` | string[] | One of query/concepts | - | 2-5 concepts for AND search (results must match ALL) |
197: | `specFolder` | string | No | - | Filter to specific spec folder |
198: | `tenantId` | string | No | - | Tenant boundary for governed retrieval |
199: | `userId` | string | No | - | User boundary for governed retrieval |
200: | `agentId` | string | No | - | Agent boundary for governed retrieval |
201: | `includeConstitutional` | boolean | No | true | Include constitutional memories |
202: | `includeContent` | boolean | No | false | Embed full file content in results |
203: | `includeContiguity` | boolean | No | false | Include adjacent memories |
204: | `anchors` | string[] | No | - | Anchor IDs to extract (e.g., `['summary', 'decisions']`) |
205: | `tier` | string | No | - | Filter by importance tier |
206: | `limit` | number | No | 10 | Maximum results to return |
207: | `useDecay` | boolean | No | true | Apply temporal decay scoring |
208: 
209: ### Intent-Aware Retrieval (7 Intents)
210: 
211: The system detects query intent and applies task-specific search weights. Seven intent types are supported:
212: 
213: | Intent | Weight Adjustments |
214: |--------|-------------------|
215: | `add_feature` | +procedural, +architectural |
216: | `fix_bug` | +error logs, +debug, +recent |
217: | `refactor` | +patterns, +structure |
218: | `security_audit` | +security, +vulnerabilities |
219: | `understand` | +semantic, +explanatory |
220: | `find_spec` | +spec documents, +plans, +decision records |
221: | `find_decision` | +decision records, +architectural context |
222: 
223: ### Constitutional Memory Behavior
224: 
225: > **Important:** Constitutional memories ALWAYS appear at the top of search results, even when a `specFolder` filter is applied. This is BY DESIGN to ensure critical context (e.g., Gate enforcement rules) is never accidentally filtered out.
226: 
227: | Parameter | Behavior |
228: |-----------|----------|
229: | `specFolder: "007-auth"` | Filters results to that folder, but constitutional memories still appear first |
230: | `includeConstitutional: false` | Explicitly excludes constitutional memories from results |
231: | `includeContent: true` | Embeds full indexed document content in results (eliminates separate load calls) |
232: 
233: ### Usage Examples
234: 
235: ```javascript
236: // Basic semantic search (query required)
237: memory_search({ query: "authentication decisions" })
238: 
239: // Folder-scoped with content (query still required)
240: memory_search({
241:   query: "OAuth implementation",
242:   specFolder: "007-auth",
243:   includeContent: true
244: })
245: 
246: // Multi-concept AND search (alternative to query)
247: memory_search({
248:   concepts: ["authentication", "session management"],
249:   specFolder: "007-auth"
250: })
251: 
252: // Exclude constitutional tier
253: memory_search({
254:   query: "login flow",
255:   includeConstitutional: false
256: })
257: 
258: // WRONG: specFolder alone is NOT sufficient
259: // memory_search({ specFolder: "007-auth" })  // ERROR: E040
260: ```
261: 
262: ### Anchor-Based Retrieval (Token-Efficient)
263: 
264: The `anchors` parameter enables **targeted section retrieval** from indexed documents, reducing token usage by ~90% when you only need specific sections.
265: 
266: **When to Use Anchors:**
267: - You need only specific sections (summary, decisions) not full content
268: - Token efficiency is important (large packet docs)
269: - Loading context for specific purposes (e.g., resume work, review decisions)
270: 
271: **Common Anchor Patterns:**
272: 
273: | Anchor ID | Content | Use Case |
274: |-----------|---------|----------|
275: | `summary` | High-level overview | Quick context refresh |
276: | `decisions` | Key decisions made | Understanding rationale |
277: | `metadata` | File metadata, dates, status | Filtering and sorting |
278: | `state` | Project state snapshot | Resume work |
279: | `context` | Project context | Understand scope |
280: | `artifacts` | Modified/created files | Track changes |
281: | `blockers` | Current blockers | Identify issues |
282: | `next-steps` | Planned actions | Continue work |
283: 
284: **Anchor Usage Examples:**
285: 
286: ```javascript
287: // Get only summary and decisions (minimal tokens)
288: memory_search({
289:   query: "auth implementation",
290:   anchors: ['summary', 'decisions']
291: })
292: 
293: // Resume work - get state and next steps
294: memory_search({
295:   query: "session context",
296:   specFolder: "007-auth",
297:   anchors: ['state', 'next-steps', 'blockers']
298: })
299: 
300: // Review what changed - artifacts only
301: memory_search({
302:   query: "recent changes",
303:   anchors: ['artifacts', 'metadata']
304: })
305: 
306: // Full content (no anchors - default behavior)
307: memory_search({
308:   query: "complete context",
309:   includeContent: true  // Returns entire file
310: })
311: ```
312: 
313: **Anchor Format in Indexed Continuity Sources:**
314: 
315: Indexed continuity-bearing docs and generated support artifacts use HTML comment anchors:
316: ```markdown
317: Brief summary of the session...
318: 
319: - Decision 1: Chose approach X because...
320: - Decision 2: Deferred Y until...
321: ```
322: 
323: **Token Savings:**
324: - Full indexed document: ~2000 tokens
325: - With `anchors: ['summary']`: ~150 tokens (93% savings)
326: - With `anchors: ['summary', 'decisions']`: ~300 tokens (85% savings)
327: 
328: ---
329: 
330: ## 5. MEMORY_LIST() BEHAVIOR
331: 
332: ### Exact Matching Behavior
333: 
334: > **Important:** The `specFolder` filter uses **EXACT matching**, not prefix or hierarchical matching.
335: 
336: | Query | Matches | Does NOT Match |
337: |-------|---------|----------------|
338: | `specFolder: "003-memory"` | `003-memory` only | `003-memory-and-spec-kit`, `003-memory-upgrade` |
339: | `specFolder: "003-memory-and-spec-kit"` | `003-memory-and-spec-kit` only | `003-memory` |
340: 
341: Use exact folder names when filtering. This is intentional for precise filtering control.
342: 
343: ### Parameter Reference
344: 
345: | Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
346: |-----------|------|---------|-------------|
347: | `specFolder` | string | - | Filter by exact spec folder name |
348: | `limit` | number | 20 | Maximum results (max 100) |
349: | `offset` | number | 0 | Pagination offset |
350: | `sortBy` | string | `created_at` DESC | Sort order: created_at, updated_at, importance_weight |
351: | `includeChunks` | boolean | `false` | Include chunk child rows. Default returns parent memories only for cleaner browsing |
352: 
353: ### Parent vs Chunk Rows
354: 
355: - By default, `memory_list()` filters to `parent_id IS NULL`, so dashboards and maintenance flows show one row per indexed parent document.
356: - Use `includeChunks: true` only when chunk-level diagnostics are needed.
357: 
358: ### Spec Folder Filtering
359: 
360: The `specFolder` parameter behavior varies by operation:
361: - `memory_search`: Exact match (SQL `=` operator)
362: - `memory_list`: Exact match (SQL `=` operator)
363: - `findMemoryFiles`: Prefix match (`startsWith`) - matches `007-auth` and `007-auth-v2`
364: 
365: For consistent exact matching, use the full spec folder name.
366: 
367: ---
368: 
369: ## 6. DECAY SCORING
370: 
371: > [VERIFIED: matches source code as of 2026-02-08]
372: 
373: The indexed-continuity store uses two complementary decay models, both **day-based** (calendar time), not turn-based.
374: 
375: ### Model A: Tier-Based Exponential Decay
376: 
377: **Source:** `lib/cognitive/attention-decay.ts:96-106`
378: 
379: **Formula:** `score_decayed = score ├Ś decayRate^(elapsedDays / 30)`
380: 
381: Where `elapsedDays` = calendar days since the spec-doc record's `updated_at` (or `created_at`). The 30-day normalization means the decay rate applies per 30-day period. Scores below `0.001` are clamped to 0.
382: 
383: #### Tier-Specific Decay Rates
384: 
385: | Tier | Decay Rate | Behavior |
386: |------|------------|----------|
387: | `constitutional` | 1.0 | Never decays (exempt) |
388: | `critical` | 1.0 | Never decays (exempt) |
389: | `important` | 1.0 | Never decays (exempt) |
390: | `normal` | 0.80 | Standard decay (~20% loss per 30 days) |
391: | `temporary` | 0.60 | Fast decay (~40% loss per 30 days) |
392: | `deprecated` | 1.0 | Never decays (but excluded from search results) |
393: 
394: **Protected Tiers:** Constitutional, critical, important, and deprecated tiers have rate = 1.0, so `decayRate^(elapsedDays/30)` = 1.0 always.
395: 
396: #### Decay Examples (Normal Tier, decay_rate = 0.80)
397: 
398: | Days Elapsed | Decay Factor (`0.80^(days/30)`) | Effective Score (base 0.50) |
399: |--------------|----------------------------------|----------------------------|
400: | 0 | 1.000 | 0.500 |
401: | 30 | 0.800 | 0.400 |
402: | 60 | 0.640 | 0.320 |
403: | 90 | 0.512 | 0.256 |
404: | 180 | 0.262 | 0.131 |
405: 
406: ### Model B: FSRS Power-Law Retrievability
407: 
408: **Source:** `lib/cognitive/attention-decay.ts:111-118`, `lib/scoring/composite-scoring.ts:123-125,158-181`
409: 
410: Used for composite scoring and the 5-state model (Section 10). Based on the FSRS v4 spaced-repetition formula:
411: 
412: **Formula:** `R = (1 + FSRS_FACTOR ├Ś t/S)^(-0.5)`
413: 
414: Where:
415: - `R` = retrievability (0 to 1)
416: - `t` = elapsed days since last review
417: - `S` = stability (learned from review history, default 1.0)
418: - `FSRS_FACTOR` = `19/81 Ōēł 0.235` (in composite-scoring) or `1/19` inline fallback (in attention-decay)
419: - Power exponent = `-0.5`
420: 
421: The FSRS model produces a power-law curve (slower initial decay, steeper long-term decay) compared to the exponential Model A. It is used by `calculateRetrievabilityScore()` in composite scoring and by the tier classifier for state transitions.
422: 
423: ### Disabling Decay
424: 
425: ```javascript
426: memory_search({ 
427:   query: "historical decisions",
428:   useDecay: false  // Returns results without temporal weighting
429: })
430: ```
431: 
432: ---
433: 
434: ## 7. REAL-TIME SYNC
435: 
436: ### Current Limitation
437: 
438: > **Note:** By default, indexed markdown is discovered on MCP server startup. Changes made after startup are not automatically detected unless the optional `SPECKIT_FILE_WATCHER` feature is enabled.
439: 
440: ### Workarounds
441: 
442: To index new or modified continuity-bearing docs:
443: 
444: 1. **Single file:** Use `memory_save` tool to index a specific file
445:    ```javascript
446:    memory_save({ filePath: "specs/007-auth/implementation-summary.md" })
447:    ```
448: 
449: 2. **Batch scan:** Use `memory_index_scan` tool to scan and index all affected docs
450:    ```javascript
451:    memory_index_scan({ specFolder: "007-auth" })
452:    ```
453: 
454: 3. **Enable watcher:** Set `SPECKIT_FILE_WATCHER=true` to opt into automatic markdown re-indexing after startup
455: 4. **Full restart:** Restart the MCP server (indexes all files on startup)
456: 
457: ### Future Enhancement
458: 
459: Real-time file watching is optional rather than always-on. By default, use `memory_save()` or `memory_index_scan()` to index new or modified files. Enable `SPECKIT_FILE_WATCHER=true` when you want automatic markdown re-indexing after startup.
460: 
461: ### Rate Limiting
462: 
463: The `memory_index_scan` operation is self-maintaining. Overlapping scans coalesce into a successful `coalesced: true` envelope instead of failing with the old cooldown error, and rows become BM25/FTS-searchable while embeddings drain in the background.
464: 
465: ---
466: 
467: ## 8. CONSTITUTIONAL RULES
468: 
469: ### Purpose
470: 
471: Constitutional rules are critical context that should surface in every relevant interaction. Examples:
472: 
473: - Gate 3 enforcement ("always ask spec folder question")
474: - Project-specific constraints
475: - Security policies
476: 
477: ### Location
478: 
479: Constitutional files are stored in:
480: ```
481: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/constitutional/
482: ```
483: 
484: ### Auto-Surfacing
485: 
486: - Indexed automatically on MCP server startup
487: - Always appear at TOP of search results
488: - Matched via trigger phrases for fast keyword matching
489: - Not affected by decay scoring
490: 
491: ### Review Cadence
492: 
493: Constitutional rules are fixed-visibility records, so operators should review them on a calendar cadence instead of relying on decay. Each active rule file should carry `last_confirmed` and `last_confirmed_source` frontmatter fields. Use the standalone staleness diagnostic to list active rules by age:
494: 
495: ```bash
496: node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/constitutional-rule-staleness.cjs
497: ```
498: 
499: Default cadence: review any rule older than 180 days. The diagnostic computes `review_by` at report time and performs no writes. A stale result is a human review signal only; it must not auto-delete, auto-demote, or change the always-surface behavior.
500: 
501: ### Creating Constitutional Rules
502: 
503: 1. Create file in `constitutional/` folder
504: 2. Add YAML frontmatter with triggers and review metadata:
505:     ```yaml
506:     ---
507:     title: Gate 3 Enforcement
508:     importanceTier: constitutional
509:     contextType: decision
510:     last_confirmed: "2026-06-10"
511:     last_confirmed_source: "human-review"
512:     triggerPhrases:
513:       - "file modification"
514:       - "gate 3"
515:       - "spec folder"
516:     ---
517:     ```
518: 3. Restart MCP server or use `memory_index_scan()`
519: 
520: ---
521: 
522: ## 9. SESSION DEDUPLICATION
523: 
524: > [VERIFIED: matches source code as of 2026-02-08]
525: 
526: The indexed-continuity store uses content hashing and session-aware deduplication to prevent redundant memories and reduce token usage.
527: 
528: ### Content Hashing
529: 
530: **Source:** `lib/parsing/memory-parser.ts:347-349`
531: 
532: Each indexed continuity source is hashed using SHA-256 of its **raw content** (no normalization):
533: 
534: ```typescript
535: function computeContentHash(content: string): string {
536:   return crypto.createHash('sha256').update(content, 'utf-8').digest('hex');
537: }
538: ```
539: 
540: This hash is stored as `content_hash` in the database and used by `memory_save()` to detect whether a file has changed ŌĆö if the hash matches, re-embedding is skipped.
541: 
542: ### Session Memory Hashing
543: 
544: **Source:** `lib/session/session-manager.ts:230-249`
545: 
546: For session deduplication, a separate hash identifies individual memories within a session:
547: 
548: ```typescript
549: function generateMemoryHash(memory: MemoryInput): string {
550:   let hashInput: string;
551: 
552:   if (memory.content_hash) {
553:     hashInput = memory.content_hash;
554:   } else if (memory.id !== undefined) {
555:     hashInput = `${memory.id}:${memory.anchorId || ''}:${memory.file_path || ''}`;
556:   } else {
557:     hashInput = JSON.stringify({
558:       anchor: memory.anchorId,
559:       path: memory.file_path,
560:       title: memory.title,

---

description: Detailed documentation for Spec Kit Memory MCP tools, behavior notes, and configuration
trigger_phrases:

# Memory System Reference - MCP Tools & Behavior

Spec Kit Memory MCP tools, behavior notes, and configuration options.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

Current baseline: schema v37 (`document_type`, `spec_level`, trigger embeddings, provenance `source_kind`, idempotency receipts, near-duplicate hints and tombstone partitions), 2 active indexed content sources plus retired compatibility artifacts, 7 intent types, and `includeSpecDocs: true` by default.

The Spec Kit Memory system provides context preservation across sessions through vector-based semantic search and packet-first continuity. Phase 018 makes `handover.md -> _memory.continuity -> spec docs` the canonical recovery chain; retired `[spec]/memory/*.md` artifacts are no longer produced at save time and only matter when older packets still contain them. This reference covers MCP tool behavior, importance tiers, decay scoring, and configuration.

When a save mutates indexed state, the runtime also updates the `DB_UPDATED_FILE` marker from `mcp_server/Level sourceconfig.ts`. Long-lived MCP processes poll that marker with `checkDatabaseUpdated()` so canonical-doc writes become visible without restarting the server.

### Architecture

| Component | Location | Purpose |
|-----------|----------|---------|
| MCP Server | `mcp_server/context-server.ts` | Spec Kit Memory MCP with vector search |
| CLI Shim | `.opencode/bin/spec-memory.cjs` | Daemon-backed CLI over the same tool surface; additive warm-only fallback when runtime MCP transport is unavailable (exit `75` = retryable daemon/IPC unavailability) |
| Database | `mcp_server/database/context-index__*.sqlite` | SQLite with FTS5 + vector embeddings; active profile filename resolved by `shared/embeddings/profile.ts:resolveActiveProfileDbPath` |
| Constitutional | `constitutional/` | Always-surface rules (Gate 3 enforcement) |
| Scripts | runtime `scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js` (source: `scripts/memory/generate-context.ts`) | Canonical continuity save entrypoint for packet docs |

### Core Support

- **Semantic search** - Find indexed spec-doc records by meaning, not just keywords
- **Importance tiers** - Six-level system for prioritizing context
- **Decay scoring** - Recent indexed spec-doc records rank higher than older ones
- **Checkpoints** - Save/restore indexed-continuity state snapshots
- **Constitutional tier** - Critical rules that always surface

### Indexable Content Sources

The indexed-continuity store indexes content from two active sources, plus a retired-compatibility row preserved for read-side retrieval against historical packets:

| Source | Location Pattern | Memory Type | Default Tier | Discovery |
|--------|-----------------|-------------|--------------|-----------|
| **Spec Documents** | `specs/**/*.md` and `<active-spec-folder>/**/*.md` | Per-type (spec, plan, tasks, etc.) | `normal` | `findSpecDocuments()` |
| **Constitutional Rules** | `.opencode/skills/*/constitutional/*.md` | `meta-cognitive` | `constitutional` | `findConstitutionalFiles()` |
| **Retired Compatibility Artifacts** | Older `specs/*/memory/*.{md,txt}` files already present in historical packets | Varies (episodic, procedural, etc.) | `normal` | Historical compatibility only |

**Content Source Behavior:**

- **Spec Documents** ŌĆö Canonical packet continuity source. Recovery should read `handover.md`, then `_memory.continuity`, then the rest of the packet docs before widening into search.
- **Constitutional Rules** ŌĆö Always-surface critical rules. Injected at top of every search result. No decay.
- **Retired Compatibility Artifacts** ŌĆö Older session notes may still exist in historical packets, but save workflows no longer produce them and operators should not treat them as an active surface.

**Spec Document Indexing Pipeline:**
1. `findSpecDocuments()` walks both supported specs roots and discovers supported doc filenames
2. `isMemoryFile()` validates each document as an indexable file
3. `extractDocumentType()` infers `document_type` (spec/plan/tasks/etc.) for scoring
4. Documents are indexed alongside memory and constitutional files
5. `createSpecDocumentChain()` links lifecycle docs for causal traversal

---

## 2. IMPORTANCE TIERS

Six-tier system for prioritizing memory relevance:

| Tier | Weight | searchBoost | Purpose | Auto-Surface |
|------|--------|-------------|---------|--------------|
| **Constitutional** | 1.0 | 3.0 | Critical rules that ALWAYS apply | Yes (top of every search) |
| **Critical** | 1.0 | 2.0 | High-importance context | Yes (high relevance) |
| **Important** | 0.8 | 1.5 | Significant decisions/context | Relevance-based |
| **Normal** | 0.5 | 1.0 | Standard session context | Relevance-based |
| **Temporary** | 0.3 | 0.5 | Short-term notes | Relevance-based |
| **Deprecated** | 0.1 | 0.0 | Outdated (kept for history) | Never (excluded from search) |

**searchBoost Multipliers:** Applied to search scores to prioritize higher tiers:
- Constitutional memories get 3x boost in search ranking
- Critical memories get 2x boost
- Important memories get 1.5x boost
- Normal memories have no boost (1.0x)
- Temporary and Deprecated get reduced visibility (0.5x and 0x respectively)

### Constitutional Tier Behavior

- Stored in `constitutional/` folder
- Auto-indexed on MCP server startup
- **ALWAYS** surface at top of search results, regardless of query
- Used for gate enforcement (e.g., "always ask spec folder question")
- EXEMPT from decay scoring (always max relevance)

---

## 3. MCP TOOLS

> **Note:** MCP tool names use plain names such as `memory_search`, `memory_save`, and `checkpoint_create`.

### Tool Reference (37 `mk-spec-memory` tools)

The public surface is 37 local descriptors in `TOOL_DEFINITIONS` from `mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts`.
Code Graph and Skill Advisor descriptors are exposed by their own MCP servers, not this registry.

| Layer | Tool | Purpose | Example Use |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| L1: Orchestration | `memory_context()` | Unified entry point with intent-aware routing (7 intents) | START HERE for most memory operations |
| L1: Orchestration | `session_resume()` | Resume memory, code graph, and Code Graph state in one call | Detailed recovery payload after reconnect, or when you want direct merged resume state |
| L1: Orchestration | `session_bootstrap()` | Composite bootstrap combining resume and health checks | Canonical first tool call in a fresh OpenCode-style session or after `/clear` |
| L2: Core | `memory_search()` | Semantic search with vector similarity | Find prior decisions on auth |
| L2: Core | `memory_quick_search()` | Simplified search wrapper for fast lookups | Quick keyword-based retrieval |
| L2: Core | `memory_match_triggers()` | Fast keyword matching (<50ms) with cognitive features | Gate enforcement |
| L2: Core | `memory_save()` | Index a saved continuity artifact or spec doc. Re-generates embedding when **content hash** changes. Title-only changes do not trigger re-embedding. | After generate-context.js |
| L3: Discovery | `memory_list()` | Browse stored spec-doc records with pagination (parent rows by default) | Review session history |
| L3: Discovery | `memory_stats()` | Get memory system statistics with composite scoring | Check index health |
| L3: Discovery | `memory_health()` | Check health status of memory system | Diagnose issues |
| L3: Discovery | `session_health()` | Report session readiness, graph freshness, and priming status | Detect stale session context before continuing |
| L4: Mutation | `memory_delete()` | Delete memory by ID or bulk delete by spec folder | Remove outdated memories |
| L4: Mutation | `memory_update()` | Update memory metadata (title, tier, triggers) | Correct memory properties |
| L4: Mutation | `memory_validate()` | Mark memory as useful/not useful | Confidence scoring |
| L4: Mutation | `memory_bulk_delete()` | Bulk delete memories by spec folder with confirmation | Clean up entire spec folder memories |
| L4: Mutation | `memory_retention_sweep()` | Audit or delete expired governed spec-doc records | Retention cleanup with dry-run evidence |

| L5: Lifecycle | `checkpoint_delete()` | Delete a checkpoint | Clean up old snapshots |
